# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

require "base64"

module ActionDispatch
  module Session
    class MarshalCookieStore < CookieStore
      DEFAULT_OPTIONS = {
        key: "_session",
        httponly: true,
        secure: false,
        same_site: :lax
      }.freeze

      def initialize(app, options = {})
        options = DEFAULT_OPTIONS.merge(options || {})
        super(app, options)
      end

      private

      def unpacked_cookie_data(req)
        cookie = req.cookies[@key]
        return {} unless cookie && !cookie.empty?

        begin
          Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookie))
        rescue
          {}
        end
      end

      def write_session(req, sid, session_data, options)
        payload = Base64.strict_encode64(Marshal.dump(session_data))
        req.cookie_jar[@key] = cookie_options(options).merge(value: payload)
        sid
      end

      def delete_session(req, sid, options)
        req.cookie_jar.delete(@key, cookie_options(options))
        generate_sid
      end

      def cookie_options(options)
        opts = @default_options.dup
        opts[:path] = options[:path] if options.key?(:path)
        opts[:domain] = options[:domain] if options.key?(:domain)
        opts[:expire_after] = options[:expire_after] if options.key?(:expire_after)
        opts[:secure] = options[:secure] if options.key?(:secure)
        opts[:httponly] = options[:httponly] if options.key?(:httponly)
        opts[:same_site] = options[:same_site] if options.key?(:same_site)
        opts
      end
    end
  end
end
